question: What should you do if another driver is tailgating you?

answers.text: Change lanes to the right if it is safe and there is space.
If changing lanes is not possible, slow down gradually to let the tailgater pass.
Do not brake suddenly.
All of the above.
answers.explanation: If you are being tailgated and there is a lane to your right, safely move over to let the tailgater pass. If no lane is available, slow down gradually to encourage the tailgater to go around you. Never brake abruptly as it increases the risk of a collision.

explanation: If you are being tailgated and there is a lane to your right, safely move over to let the tailgater pass. If no lane is available, slow down gradually to encourage the tailgater to go around you. Never brake abruptly as it increases the risk of a collision.

question: What should you do if one of your tires blows out while you’re driving?

answers.text: Immediately apply the brakes firmly
Ease off the accelerator and steer straight
answers.explanation: When you experience a sudden tire blowout while driving, maintain a firm grip on the steering wheel and keep the vehicle going straight. Slow down by lifting your foot off the accelerator and avoid braking suddenly. Use gentle braking only if it’s necessary and safely move off the road once the vehicle has slowed down.

answers.text: Pump the brakes to maintain control

explanation: When you experience a sudden tire blowout while driving, maintain a firm grip on the steering wheel and keep the vehicle going straight. Slow down by lifting your foot off the accelerator and avoid braking suddenly. Use gentle braking only if it’s necessary and safely move off the road once the vehicle has slowed down.

question: Is it mandatory for a driver to make sure that minors in a vehicle are using proper safety restraints?

answers.text: True
answers.explanation: Drivers must ensure all passengers under 18 are in appropriate restraints regardless of seating position.

answers.text: False

explanation: Drivers must ensure all passengers under 18 are in appropriate restraints regardless of seating position.

question: On a highway, why is it important to look further ahead than when driving in the city?

answers.text: To anticipate upcoming hazards in advance.
answers.explanation: Looking farther ahead on a freeway allows you to recognize changes in traffic conditions, be alert for signals from other drivers, and anticipate merging vehicles or sudden alterations in traffic flow.

answers.text: Because stopping distance is very long on freeways.
To match the speed of other cars.

explanation: Looking farther ahead on a freeway allows you to recognize changes in traffic conditions, be alert for signals from other drivers, and anticipate merging vehicles or sudden alterations in traffic flow.

question: What do two solid yellow lines on the roadway signify?

answers.text: You may pass slower vehicles in either direction.
Overtaking other vehicles is prohibited in both directions.
answers.explanation: Two solid yellow lines indicate that traffic is moving in opposite directions and passing from either side is prohibited.

answers.text: You can only pass during certain hours.
Passing is permissible if the road is straight.

explanation: Two solid yellow lines indicate that traffic is moving in opposite directions and passing from either side is prohibited.

question: Which substances can impair your driving capabilities?

answers.text: Prescribed medications only
All types of medications
answers.explanation: Any type of medication, whether over-the-counter or prescribed, can impair your ability to drive. Always read the warning labels and know how the medication affects you before deciding to drive.

answers.text: Alcohol and illegal substances

explanation: Any type of medication, whether over-the-counter or prescribed, can impair your ability to drive. Always read the warning labels and know how the medication affects you before deciding to drive.

question: When should you adjust your seat and mirrors?

answers.text: Before you start the engine.
answers.explanation: Your safety and the safety of other drivers and pedestrians depend heavily on this. It is important to adjust your seat and mirrors before starting the engine to ensure proper visibility and control.

answers.text: When you notice they need adjustment.
Before you enter the vehicle.

explanation: Your safety and the safety of other drivers and pedestrians depend heavily on this. It is important to adjust your seat and mirrors before starting the engine to ensure proper visibility and control.

question: When you are in a line of cars at a stop sign, and there is no cross traffic after the car in front of you moves, you:

answers.text: Can proceed directly through the intersection without stopping again.
Must come to a complete stop at the sign or line before moving ahead.
answers.explanation: Always make a full stop at the stop sign or line, even if you are in a line of vehicles. This ensures safety and compliance with traffic laws.

answers.text: May slowly roll through the intersection.
Can follow the vehicle ahead without stopping.

explanation: Always make a full stop at the stop sign or line, even if you are in a line of vehicles. This ensures safety and compliance with traffic laws.

question: Which of the following abilities are diminished by drinking alcohol?

answers.text: Reasoning
Decision-making
Eyesight
All of the above
answers.explanation: All of these mental and physical abilities can be impaired when alcohol is consumed.

explanation: All of these mental and physical abilities can be impaired when alcohol is consumed.

question: Fatigued drivers:

answers.text: Should consume energy drinks to enhance alertness.
May drive in a manner similar to someone who is intoxicated.
answers.explanation: Drowsiness can impair driving skills to the extent that it resembles drunken driving.

answers.text: Can remain attentive by blowing cool air into the vehicle.
Only feel sleepy during long trips.

explanation: Drowsiness can impair driving skills to the extent that it resembles drunken driving.

question: You may pass another vehicle on the left side of a two-lane roadway:

answers.text: When overtaking another vehicle on a hill.
When passing another vehicle near a railroad crossing.
In a designated passing zone when the road ahead is clear.
answers.explanation: When driving on a two-lane road, ensure it is safe to pass and only in designated zones. Never pass near a hill, curve, or railroad crossing.

answers.text: When passing another vehicle in a curve.

explanation: When driving on a two-lane road, ensure it is safe to pass and only in designated zones. Never pass near a hill, curve, or railroad crossing.

question: What are common causes of traffic accidents?

answers.text: Distracted driving
Driving too fast for the current road conditions
Tailgating other vehicles
All of the above
answers.explanation: Driving too quickly for the given road conditions, being distracted, or following other vehicles too closely often leads to traffic accidents.

explanation: Driving too quickly for the given road conditions, being distracted, or following other vehicles too closely often leads to traffic accidents.

question: What does this sign warn drivers about the road ahead?

answers.text: The road makes a sharp turn to the right followed by a left turn.
The road curves to the right and then to the left.
answers.explanation: This traffic sign indicates a reverse curve where the road first curves to the right, then to the left.

answers.text: The road simply curves to the right.

explanation: This traffic sign indicates a reverse curve where the road first curves to the right, then to the left.

question: What is the equivalent traffic control requirement of a flashing red light at an intersection?

answers.text: A railroad crossing sign.
A warning sign.
A stop sign.
answers.explanation: A flashing red light at an intersection requires drivers to come to a complete stop and proceed only when it is safe, similar to a stop sign.

answers.text: An intersection sign.

explanation: A flashing red light at an intersection requires drivers to come to a complete stop and proceed only when it is safe, similar to a stop sign.

question: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop simultaneously, who should proceed first?

answers.text: The vehicle on the right goes first.
answers.explanation: When two vehicles arrive simultaneously at a four-way stop, the driver on the right has the right-of-way.

answers.text: The vehicle on the left goes first.
Both vehicles continue without stopping.
Both drivers must signal their intention by honking.

explanation: When two vehicles arrive simultaneously at a four-way stop, the driver on the right has the right-of-way.

question: What action should a driver take when encountering a flashing yellow High Intensity Activated Crosswalk (HAWK) signal?

answers.text: It is safe to continue driving without stopping.
Reduce speed and be ready to stop.
answers.explanation: A flashing yellow light on a HAWK signal alerts drivers to slow down and get ready to stop for pedestrians.

answers.text: Come to a complete halt immediately.
No need for any action unless pedestrians are visible.

explanation: A flashing yellow light on a HAWK signal alerts drivers to slow down and get ready to stop for pedestrians.

question: When making a left turn, which vehicles must you yield to?

answers.text: Vehicles approaching from the opposite direction, going straight or turning right.
answers.explanation: When making a left turn, you must yield to oncoming traffic that is traveling straight or making a right turn.

answers.text: Vehicles in adjacent lanes.
Only vehicles that are turning left.

explanation: When making a left turn, you must yield to oncoming traffic that is traveling straight or making a right turn.

question: What should you do when an emergency vehicle with flashing lights is approaching?

answers.text: Accelerate quickly to get out of the way of the emergency vehicle.
Immediately slam on your brakes to stop your vehicle.
Move out of the lane and allow the emergency vehicle to pass safely.
answers.explanation: When an emergency vehicle with flashing lights is approaching, you should move out of the way safely and provide a clear path for it to pass. If possible, pull to the side of the road.

answers.text: Wave your hands to signal the emergency vehicle.

explanation: When an emergency vehicle with flashing lights is approaching, you should move out of the way safely and provide a clear path for it to pass. If possible, pull to the side of the road.

question: When your vehicle is skidding, you should turn the steering wheel:

answers.text: Straight ahead.
Into the direction of the skid.
answers.explanation: When in a skid, steering into the direction the vehicle is sliding helps regain control.

answers.text: Against the skid.
Towards the rear of your vehicle.

explanation: When in a skid, steering into the direction the vehicle is sliding helps regain control.

question: What steps should you take when changing lanes?

answers.text: Use your turn signal.
Look in your rearview and side mirrors.
Quickly glance over your shoulder to check blind spots.
All of the above.
answers.explanation: When changing lanes, it's important to use your turn signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder to ensure safety.

explanation: When changing lanes, it's important to use your turn signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder to ensure safety.

question: What should you do if you start feeling sleepy while driving?

answers.text: Continue driving and try to stay awake.
Stop at a safe place to rest.
answers.explanation: When you feel drowsy while driving, it is crucial to find the nearest safe location, such as a rest area or a service stop, to take a break. Do not rely on stimulants like caffeine, as they do not replace the need for sleep and can still leave you impaired.

answers.text: Drink energy drinks to stay alert.

explanation: When you feel drowsy while driving, it is crucial to find the nearest safe location, such as a rest area or a service stop, to take a break. Do not rely on stimulants like caffeine, as they do not replace the need for sleep and can still leave you impaired.

question: What is the impact of drinking alcohol before driving?

answers.text: Not a major concern if you feel fine.
A significant threat to road safety.
answers.explanation: Operating a vehicle under the influence of alcohol poses a serious risk to the safety of all road users. It impairs judgment, coordination, and reaction time, making safe driving impossible.

answers.text: Acceptable if done infrequently.
Only risky to those who are driving.

explanation: Operating a vehicle under the influence of alcohol poses a serious risk to the safety of all road users. It impairs judgment, coordination, and reaction time, making safe driving impossible.

question: What does a single dashed white line down the middle of a road signify?

answers.text: Traffic is moving in opposite directions, and overtaking is not allowed.
Traffic is moving in opposite directions, and passing is allowed when safe.
Traffic is traveling in the same direction, and passing is not allowed.
Traffic is moving in the same direction, and passing is allowed when safe.
answers.explanation: White dashed lines indicate that traffic flows in the same direction and passing is allowed when safe.

explanation: White dashed lines indicate that traffic flows in the same direction and passing is allowed when safe.

question: What strategies can drivers use to avoid 'Highway hypnosis'?

answers.text: Move your eyes regularly.
Take breaks after every 100 miles.
Drive no more than eight hours per day.
All of the above.
answers.explanation: Combining all these practices helps prevent highway hypnosis.

explanation: Combining all these practices helps prevent highway hypnosis.

question: You encounter a red light at an intersection, but a police officer is signaling for you to continue driving. What should you do?

answers.text: Wait for the light to change before moving.
Obey the officer's directions.
answers.explanation: Always follow the instructions of a police officer directing traffic, even if it contradicts traffic signals.

answers.text: Stop completely and then proceed with caution.

explanation: Always follow the instructions of a police officer directing traffic, even if it contradicts traffic signals.

question: What is the only effective way to lower your blood alcohol concentration (BAC)?

answers.text: Take a nap.
Eat a big meal.
Wait for time to pass.
answers.explanation: The body needs time to metabolize alcohol. No external method such as drinking coffee, taking a shower, or exercising will speed up this process.

explanation: The body needs time to metabolize alcohol. No external method such as drinking coffee, taking a shower, or exercising will speed up this process.

question: Which color is typically used for guide signs that show distances and directions?

answers.text: Yellow
Green
answers.explanation: Green signs show directions and distances to various locations.

answers.text: Red
Blue

explanation: Green signs show directions and distances to various locations.

question: You are allowed to overtake another vehicle on a two-lane road when you are within 100 feet of a junction or railway crossing.

answers.text: True
False
answers.explanation: Passing is forbidden within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing on a two-lane, two-way road.

explanation: Passing is forbidden within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing on a two-lane, two-way road.

question: What factors can impair a person's driving abilities?

answers.text: Illegal drugs.
Prescription drugs.
Fatigue.
All of the above.
answers.explanation: Safe driving requires a driver to be in good physical and mental condition. Impairment can be caused by illegal substances, over-the-counter and prescription medications, as well as by being tired.

explanation: Safe driving requires a driver to be in good physical and mental condition. Impairment can be caused by illegal substances, over-the-counter and prescription medications, as well as by being tired.

question: What should you do before making a turn?

answers.text: Signal your turn.
answers.explanation: To safely execute a turn, you must signal your intent to turn at least three to four seconds beforehand.

answers.text: Steer into the turn.
Speed up.
Switch lanes.

explanation: To safely execute a turn, you must signal your intent to turn at least three to four seconds beforehand.

question: When are roads most likely to be particularly slippery?

answers.text: During heavy rain after several hours.
The morning after a rainstorm.
During the first few minutes of rain.
answers.explanation: The initial minutes of a rainstorm can make roads particularly slick due to oils rising to the surface, creating a hazardous driving condition.

answers.text: Immediately after the rain stops.

explanation: The initial minutes of a rainstorm can make roads particularly slick due to oils rising to the surface, creating a hazardous driving condition.

question: After overtaking another vehicle, when is it safe to return to your lane?

answers.text: When you have safely moved past the other vehicle.
When you receive a hand signal from the other driver.
When the entire vehicle you passed is visible in your rearview mirror.
answers.explanation: Ensure that the entire vehicle you've overtaken is clearly visible in your rearview mirror before returning to the original lane.

answers.text: When you are beside the other vehicle.

explanation: Ensure that the entire vehicle you've overtaken is clearly visible in your rearview mirror before returning to the original lane.

question: Is it mandatory to use signals when pulling out of a parking space?

answers.text: True
answers.explanation: Drivers should always signal to alert others before exiting a parking spot to prevent accidents.

answers.text: False

explanation: Drivers should always signal to alert others before exiting a parking spot to prevent accidents.

question: Where is the safest position to place a child safety seat?

answers.text: The left rear seat.
The front seat beside the driver.
The middle of the rear seat.
answers.explanation: For optimal safety, child safety seats should be placed in the center of the back seat.

answers.text: Anywhere in the vehicle.

explanation: For optimal safety, child safety seats should be placed in the center of the back seat.
